title: The Loss That Stuck
description: You replayed a loss in your head for two days. Adjustments planned.

choices.text: Break down what went wrong and fix it
choices.flavor: You drew plays on notebook paper. The Xs and Os do not quite make sense yet, but the instinct is there.

choices.text: Use the anger as fuel for next time
choices.flavor: The loss burns. Good. That fire is useful.

choices.text: Shake it off and move on
choices.flavor: It is one game. There will be more. Healthy perspective at a young age.

title: Picked First
description: For the first time, you got picked first in a playground game. Both captains wanted you.

choices.flavor: You walk over like this happens every day. It does not. But it might start.

choices.text: Pick the weaker team to prove a point
choices.flavor: Bold move. Your team is worse on paper. You carry them. Everyone remembers.

title: Choosing Harder Opponents
description: You could play with kids your age or join the older kids' game. The older kids hit harder.

choices.flavor: You get knocked around. But you hold your own. The older kids start picking you first.
Easy wins feel good but something is missing. You are the big fish in a small pond.

title: The Bad Day
description: You dropped an easy catch in front of everyone. They laughed. Your face is on fire.

choices.text: Go home and practice catches until your hands hurt
choices.flavor: 200 catches against the wall that evening. You will never drop that ball again.

choices.text: Laugh it off and make a joke
choices.flavor: You turn it into a bit. The laughing shifts from at you to with you. Recovery complete.

choices.text: Avoid playing for a few days
choices.flavor: The sting fades. You come back next week. But the memory lingers.

title: Captain Material
description: The game is falling apart. Kids are arguing about calls. Someone needs to take charge.

choices.text: Settle the argument and get the game moving
choices.flavor: You make the call. Both sides grumble. But they play. That is what leaders do.

choices.text: Pick sides and back your friend
choices.flavor: Loyalty is good. But picking favorites as a leader creates problems.
